title: Cucumber and aloe vera melt-and-pour soap
subtitle: A fresh, cooling bar made with aloe vera gel and cucumber fragrance in a clear glycerine base
excerpt: A clear melt-and-pour glycerine base lets in aloe vera gel and a cucumber fragrance without any lye handling. The bar is cooling and mild, made in under an hour, and ready to use after 4 hours of setting time. Suitable for normal to sensitive skin.
sourceNotes: Melt-and-pour glycerine soap base: a pre-saponified soap base containing glycerine and surfactant. The Soapmaker's Companion by Cavitch (1997) covers M&P as the beginner route to soap making. Aloe vera gel as a soap additive: maximum 10% of base weight for a clear M&P base; higher percentages cloud the base and may cause separation. Cucumber fragrance oils for soap: IFRA-rated for rinse-off products.

recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe to fill a larger mould or make a second tray. Proportions hold at double batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bars are ready to use after 4 hours; the base is fully set. For gift packaging, allow 24 hours.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months wrapped in cling film or in an airtight container. Melt-and-pour bars absorb moisture from the air (glycerine is hygroscopic) and develop surface beads if left uncovered in a humid room. Discard if the bar develops mould, an off smell, or an unusually slimy surface.

content.text: Melt-and-pour soap uses a pre-made glycerine base that has already been processed into soap; there is no lye to handle. The method is to melt the base, stir in additions, and pour into a mould. This cucumber and aloe vera bar is cooling and mild, with a clear glycerine base that shows the pale green tint of the aloe vera gel. The aloe adds a skin-soothing quality and keeps at 5% of the base weight to maintain clarity. A cucumber fragrance oil gives a fresh, clean scent.

items.prepNote: Clear glycerine melt-and-pour soap base; available from soap suppliers
5% of base weight; stabilised cosmetic aloe vera gel
Cucumber-scented cosmetic fragrance oil at around 2% of base weight

content.text: Cut or break the melt-and-pour base into small chunks. Melt in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water (a double boiler), stirring occasionally. Do not overheat; the base should melt at around 55 to 65°C. Do not boil, as this drives off the glycerine and leaves the bar brittle. Use the thermometer to monitor the temperature and remove from the heat as soon as the base is fully liquid.
Add the aloe vera and fragrance

Allow the melted base to cool slightly to around 55°C. Stir in the aloe vera gel until fully incorporated; it will cloud the base slightly at the point of addition but should clear as it blends. Add the cucumber fragrance oil and stir gently. Avoid whipping air into the base.

Pour the liquid base into the silicone loaf mould or individual bar moulds. Spritz the surface with isopropyl alcohol to pop any surface bubbles before the base sets. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for at least 4 hours. Do not refrigerate; the glycerine draws moisture and the surface sweats in a cold environment.
Unmould the bar or individual bars once fully set. The silicone mould flexes to release cleanly. Wrap each bar in cling film immediately to prevent surface beading from humidity.

Keep wrapped bars in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight. Glycerine soap bases are hygroscopic: unwrapped bars in a bathroom collect surface moisture beads (glycerine dew). This is harmless but messy. Wrapped, the bars keep 12 months. Discard if mould appears, if the bar smells off, or if the surface becomes unusually slimy rather than simply beaded.
